name: Roast Pork Loin with Sage Crust and Apple Bra
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Fresh sage, woody stems removed, chopped (1 oz)
2 medium Apples, tart (Granny Smith or Braeburn), cored
1/4 cup Fresh basil leaves, chopped (about 1/2 cup)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine sage, garlic, olive oil, and peppercorns in a shallow glass or non-metal dish. Pat the pork loin with the sage-garlic mixture on all sides. Cover and marinate in the refrigerator for at least 8 hours or overnight.
2. Place the pork on a rack in a roasting pan. Roast in a preheated 450°F oven for 15 minutes. Lower the oven temperature to 350°F and roast until the internal temperature reaches 160°F, about 25-30 more minutes.
3. Transfer meat to a hot platter. Cover with aluminum foil or place in a warm oven to keep warm. Let the meat rest for several minutes before slicing.
4. Meanwhile, add 1/4 cup of hot stock to the pan. Scrape up browned bits from the bottom of the pan and skim off any fat. Pour drippings into a measuring cup.
5. To make the sauce: Chop apples into 1-inch cubes. Heat olive oil in a medium saucepan. Add shallots and sauté over medium heat until lightly browned, about 3-5 minutes. Add apples and basil, stirring to combine. Add Calvados and raise the heat to medium-high.
6. Ignite the sauce with a long match (or pour Calvados into a heat-safe ladle and ignite, then pour into the pan). Stir in the pan drippings and remaining veal stock; continue cooking over low heat for 15 minutes to reduce. Dissolve cornstarch in cold water and stir it into the pan. Bring the mixture to a full boil.
7. Cook, stirring often, until the sauce thickens. Remove from heat and season with salt and pepper. Serve immediately alongside pork.
Note: Defatted chicken stock or chicken broth may be substituted for the veal stock. Frozen concentrated stock is available at specialty markets. Brandy or Armagnac may be substituted for Calvados.
